novel_features.name: Saved-search watch with diff
novel_features.description: Re-run a saved gis search and surface what's NEW, REMOVED, PRICE-CHANGED, or STATUS-CHANGED since the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin's saved-search emails show only new listings — no removal alerts, no price-drop alerts, no status transitions. The diff requires snapshot history only this CLI keeps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent is tracking a buyer's shortlist over time and needs a reproducible 'what changed' digest.

novel_features.name: $/sqft net-HOA ranking
novel_features.description: Rank synced listings by price-per-sqft, with optional HOA-fee subtraction over a 5-year horizon.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin's sort options are price-only; net-HOA $/sqft is the metric small-portfolio investors actually screen by, and no scraper or API exposes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when value-per-dollar is the goal and HOA-heavy condos must compete fairly against single-family.

novel_features.name: Side-by-side compare
novel_features.description: Pull 2-8 listings through the combined Stingray detail endpoint and emit aligned columnar output (price, $/sqft, beds, baths, lot, year, schools, AVM delta, last sale, taxes).
novel_features.rationale: Redfin has no compare view. Aligning fields across listings lives only in the local-store pivot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when narrowing a shortlist; the wide table makes school-rating and AVM-delta differences obvious.

novel_features.name: Sold-comp recipe
novel_features.description: For a subject listing, derive a circular polygon from --radius, run a sold-status search, filter by --sqft-tol and --bed-match, return the ranked comp set.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin's polygon tool is clumsy and resets between sessions. Saving a 'comp recipe' as flags lives only in a CLI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to pull comparable sales for a buyer offer; collapses 20 minutes of polygon-clicking into one command.

novel_features.name: Stale + price-drop scan
novel_features.description: List active listings whose price dropped by N% in a window, OR whose days-on-market exceed a threshold.
novel_features.rationale: DOM is buried inside Redfin's listing pages and not exposed in search results; price-drop history is a per-listing lookup. Both are time-series queries against the local snapshot table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when timing the market or surfacing lowball candidates before tour scheduling.

novel_features.name: Multi-region union ranking
novel_features.description: Union synced listings across multiple region slugs and rank across the entire set, deduped by listing URL.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin's UI is single-region per query; cross-region union is the relocator and multi-zip investor view that the website cannot produce.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs a single ranked feed across multiple metros without writing a fan-out loop.

novel_features.name: Trends overlay across regions
novel_features.description: Pull aggregate-trends for N regions and emit one tidy long table (region × month × metric) over a window.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin's market-trends page shows one region at a time; cross-region overlay over the same N-month window requires joining N API calls into one tidy frame.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a relocator is comparing cities and needs the medians overlaid on the same axis.

novel_features.name: Bulk export past the 350 cap
novel_features.description: Slice the price space into bands, page-walk gis-csv per band until each returns under 350 rows, dedupe on listing URL, emit one CSV/JSON.
novel_features.rationale: The 350-row gis-csv cap is the most-cited Redfin scraper limitation. Adaptive band-slicing fixes it; community scrapers reimplement this badly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when you need every comp for a year, not the first 350 sorted by relevance.

novel_features.name: Neighborhood market summary
novel_features.description: Single command: active count, pending count, sold-90d count, median list, median sold, median DOM, median $/sqft, % with price drops, plus a trends snapshot.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin spreads these stats across four pages; the join across local store and trends endpoint lives only in the CLI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the one-shot snapshot of a market for a buyer brief.

novel_features.name: Region appreciation ranker
novel_features.description: For all child neighborhoods under a parent metro, call aggregate-trends and rank by YoY median-sale % change.
novel_features.rationale: Redfin shows trends per region but never ranks neighborhoods within a metro by appreciation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a relocator or investor needs the 'where in this metro is hottest' answer.

narrative.headline: Stingray-backed Redfin CLI with the workflows the website can't do — saved-search diff, sold-price trends, $/sqft ranking, and offline SQL.
narrative.value_prop: Search homes for sale via Redfin's internal Stingray endpoints from the terminal, sync results to a local SQLite store, and run the workflows the website never built: diff a saved search week-over-week with `watch`, rank by $/sqft net of HOA with `rank`, pull sold comps for a subject property with `comps`, surface price drops or stale listings with `drops`, and overlay market trends across multiple cities with `trends`. Every command is `--json` / `--select`-shaped so an agent can pipe the output without burning context.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. The Stingray endpoints are the same ones Redfin's web app uses, served unauthenticated to US IPs. Account-only features (saved homes, alerts, agent dashboard) are intentionally out of scope. Surf with Chrome TLS fingerprint clears AWS-WAF protection at runtime; cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter handles per-IP rate limits with exponential backoff.

quickstart.comment: First — resolve the canonical region_id you need for every search.
Search — verifies Stingray endpoints respond and JSON output is well-formed.
Persist that search to the local store under slug 'austin-3br'.
Rank synced listings by net-HOA $/sqft — the metric Redfin's sort never offers.
After a few days — diff against the previous sync and emit NEW / REMOVED / PRICE-CHANGED / STATUS-CHANGED.
One-shot market snapshot: active, pending, sold-90d, medians, % with price drops.

troubleshoots.fix: Surf cleared the homepage but Redfin rate-limits per IP. Run `redfin-pp-cli doctor` and wait 30-60 seconds before retrying; cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter will back off automatically.

troubleshoots.symptom: All commands return 403 from non-US IPs
troubleshoots.fix: Stingray is geo-restricted to US IPs. Run from a US-based machine, or use a US VPN.

troubleshoots.symptom: watch reports no changes when you know listings changed
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm a previous `sync-search` exists. Check with: `sqlite3 ~/.local/share/redfin-pp-cli/data.db "SELECT MAX(observed_at) FROM listing_snapshots WHERE saved_search = 'austin-3br'"`. If empty, run sync first.
These read from the local store. Run `sync-search` first. Check with: `sqlite3 ~/.local/share/redfin-pp-cli/data.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing"`.

troubleshoots.symptom: export hits the 350-row cap on a single price band
troubleshoots.fix: The bulk exporter slices the price space automatically. If a band is still saturated, narrow `--status` or split by `--year`.

troubleshoots.symptom: JSON parse error: unexpected token at offset 0
troubleshoots.fix: Stingray wraps responses in `{}&&{...}`. The CLI strips the prefix automatically; if you're calling the API directly, drop the first 4 bytes before parsing.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or human needs structured Redfin data and needs the workflows the website itself doesn't expose: cross-region diffs over time, $/sqft rankings net of HOA, sold-comp recipes for a subject property, market-trends overlays across cities, and digest-style summaries. Reach for it for buyer comparison-shopping, investor screening across multiple zips, relocator city comparisons, and buyer's-agent comp pulls. Skip it for one-off browsing — the redfin.com website is fine for that.

recipes.title: Three-metro relocator overlay
recipes.explanation: Pulls aggregate-trends for all three metros over the same 24-month window, joins them into one tidy long table for export — the overlay Redfin's UI never produces.

recipes.title: Buyer's-agent comp pull in one command
recipes.explanation: Resolves the subject listing, derives a 0.5-mile circle, pulls sold homes in the last 6 months matching beds and within 15% sqft, returns the ranked comp set ready for a buyer offer brief.

recipes.title: Investor screen across 5 zips
recipes.explanation: Unions synced searches across 5 Austin zips and ranks the full union by net-HOA $/sqft. Filter narrows to investor screen criteria; result is a single ranked feed Redfin cannot produce.

recipes.title: Weekly diff for a saved search
recipes.explanation: Composes the watch result with --select to emit only the columns the agent needs — agents can drop straight into a weekly buyer email without extra parsing.

recipes.title: Bulk pull sold homes for 2024
recipes.explanation: Slices the price space into bands, page-walks gis-csv per band, dedupes on listing URL across bands, emits a single CSV that exceeds Redfin's 350-row cap.

trigger_phrases: rank houses by price per square foot
